query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "fashionchameleon: towards real-time and interactive human-garment vide" computer science
rationale: The paper focuses on real-time sensor data analytics in industrial bulk materials handling and cyber-physical systems, which is an entirely different domain from the user's question about extending a computer vision framework for human-garment video interaction. It shares only the generic keyword "real-time" but addresses a distinct construct (industrial physics modeling vs. human pose/garment tracking) with no overlap in mechanism, variables, or empirical population.
